title: Spring One
description:
Welcome to the Sounds of Fylm show. In this first episode you will find a preview through sound of the first screening event later in March at Verdant Taproom. The screening on Tuesday March 24th is a celebration of Falmouth University’s Sound/Image Cinema Lab’s ongoing partnership with Kernow’s film production company par excellence, Bosena. Music in the show comes from the three films screening that night, A Year in a Field (Morris, 2023), Tresor (Gwenno, 2022) and Pen Mari (Marshall-Waters, 2025).

In future shows you will be able to hear the on-stage conversations recorded the previous month. For the inaugural sonic outing, host Neil Fox shares some of his favourite sounds from films, including his own, plus some recent soundtrack and score highlights, including a track from a record purchased at a recent Taproom record fair. Enjoy, and see you in the dark.

title: Bill Brewster
description:
On Sunday 23 September 2025, Bill played for us for the final party in Verdant’s celebrations. A music loving crowd from across Cornwall joined us for a magical evening.

Bill Brewster began DJing in the 1980s, but came into his own in the early 1990s during a two-year stint in New York running DMC’s office, where nights at the Sound Factory and hanging out with Danny Tenaglia gave him the grounding you can still hear in his music today. Bill was also one of the founding residents at Fabric in London, where he played for five years.

Bill is a key figure in the history of DJ culture and co-wrote the book Last Night a DJ Saved My Life (White Rabbit Publications) with Frank Broughton, a classic that chronicles the role of the DJ in the 20th century. With Frank, he runs DJHistory.com and the UK’s longest running underground party, Lowlife.

title: Hearts & Minds
description: We are joined by Paul Hill, a talented artist and tattooer. We delve into a new collaboration between Paul and Verdant, discussing the inspiration behind the can design and Paul’s journey as a tattoo artist. From the evolution of the concept to the intricacies of collaboration, we uncover the creative process behind Hearts and Minds. Join us as we dive deep into Paul’s fascinating world and discover the stories behind the ink.

title: Club Shipwrecked
description: © Club Shipwrecked is a collective of artists and reprobates making radio shows, podcasts, live events and more. Come get involved. Diddley-ay-ey

title: GLOK / Andy Bell
description:
Before Andy joins us at the Taproom in October he joins Matthew Shaw for a conversation about his musical journey. Via trips to Cornwall and a guitar playing uncle who taught him all the classics from The Stones and The Beatles and into The Velvet underground.

Andy Bell of pioneering shoegazers RIDE is parking his Space Station in low Earth orbit to take the GLOK-mobile for a spin to a venue near you this October. GLOKtober offers a rare chance to see Andy performing live as his electronic alter ego, where he brilliantly weaves together throbbing dubbed-out acid, levitational psychedelia, Balearic, ambient, techno, Kosmische, shoegaze, art-rock and Compass Point-style post punk, with his virtuoso guitar playing and musicianship to the fore.

title: Dance Your Way Home
description:
This book is about the kind of ordinary dancing you and I might do in our kitchens when a favourite tune comes on. It’s more than a social history: it’s a set of interconnected histories of the overlooked places where dancing happens . . .

Why do we dance together? What does dancing tells us about ourselves, individually and collectively? And what can it do for us? Whether it be at home, ’80s club nights, Irish dancehalls or reggae dances, jungle raves or volunteer-run spaces and youth centres, Emma Warren has sought the answers to these questions her entire life.

Emma Warren
An experienced author, editor, journalist and broadcaster with experience in lecturing, workshops and in youth work.

Ruth Pethybridge
Dr Ruth Pethybridge is a choreographer, facilitator and researcher. She has delivered dance in diverse settings with all ages: from babies to the elderly and everything in between.

title: Jon Hopkins
description: We caught up with Jon Hopkins on his visit to the brewery in 2023. We talk all about the beer we made with Jon, Light Through The Veins, a 9% ABV Double IPA. The design and music process behind the label and the track that inspired the beer and all about the number nine.

title: The Internal Garden
playlist:
You will never see plant life in the same way again! Sound Artist Justin Wiggan invites you to Internal Garden.

Internal Garden is an immersive sound art research performance programme of live investigation, interactive art installation, educational events and a public exhibition in context of the themes of the sound art and plant consciousness.
